stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The content points to the ongoing community interest and adoption of Meta's Llama models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: Meta confirms 1000s of Instagram accounts were hacked by abusing its AI chatbot
stories.author: speckx
bullet_points: Meta confirmed a significant security breach involving thousands of Instagram accounts.
The attack vector utilized the abuse of Meta's own AI chatbot.
This highlights critical vulnerabilities in the security implementation of large-scale AI systems.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a language model.
The associated tags suggest it utilizes modern transformer architectures and is relevant to conversational AI.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating an ongoing optimization and development effort.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging workflows with local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A small modular nuclear reactor (SMR) has achieved criticality in its initial test.
The reactor is being developed by the startup Antares.
The technology is not yet operational for power generation.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The S&P 500 is reportedly restricting passive investment access for SpaceX.
The exclusion extends to major AI players like OpenAI and Anthropic.
This suggests a growing selectivity or regulatory hurdle for high-growth, non-traditional tech companies within major indices.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Introduces Code2LoRA, a hypernetwork framework designed to inject repository-level context into Code Language Models.
Addresses the limitations of current methods (long inputs/RAG or per-repository fine-tuning) which are costly and brittle to code evolution.
Achieves repository knowledge injection with zero inference-time token overhead by generating specific LoRA adapters.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) algorithm discovery.
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, such as inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and poor hierarchical control.
Positions the research at the intersection of LLM agents and long-horizon tasks like scientific discovery and advanced ML engineering.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Introduces Code2LoRA, a hypernetwork framework designed to inject repository-level context into Code Language Models.
Addresses the limitations of current methods (long inputs/RAG or costly per-repository fine-tuning) for handling evolving codebases.
Achieves repository knowledge injection with zero inference-time token overhead by generating specific LoRA adapters.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Machine Learning Engineering (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, such as information isolation, memoryless search, and lack of hierarchical control.
Aims to enable sustained, end-to-end optimization for complex, long-horizon tasks like automated algorithm discovery.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', indicating its primary application in generative AI.
The model utilizes key ML frameworks and libraries such as transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: Meta made its own AI-generated clickbait news feed
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Meta is using its AI to generate clickbait-style news content.
The feature is integrated into the standalone Meta AI app's 'For You' section.
The generated content includes AI-created topics, images, and text.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB-connected speaker has been shown to potentially infect a PC without physical interaction.
The security behavior in question is associated with the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The product seller does not classify this behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Introduces HANDOFF, a new framework for whole-body control in humanoid robots.
Addresses the critical challenge of bridging high-level task planning with low-level robot control.
Proposes a compact, explicit, and general interface to replace dense kinematic references.

stories.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: TempoVLA addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: the inability to control execution speed dynamically.
The research tackles the need for robots to switch between fast transit phases and slow, precise contact stages during manipulation.
Unlike previous methods that only change between fixed speeds, TempoVLA explores and enables smooth deceleration in robotic policies.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in AI-text detection by focusing on the *process* of human-AI co-editing, rather than just the final output.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to track how AI authorship signals evolve during document revision.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional workflows, highlighting a critical need for better authorship attribution tools.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs).
Proposes Supervised Memory Training (SMT) to bypass the need for recurrent credit propagation.
Aims to improve long-range association learning by overcoming vanishing/exploding gradient issues inherent in RNNs.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for reasoning language models.
It proposes RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve training efficiency.
The work focuses on overcoming limitations of current methods like GRPO, which treat the entire Chain-of-Thought (CoT) as a single unit for reward assignment.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: Introduces a novel method using self-augmenting retrieval for Diffusion Language Models (DLMs).
DLMs generate text by parallel denoising, predicting tentative tokens at each step.
The discarded, low-confidence tokens serve as a valuable lookahead signal to enhance retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows preconditioned weights to be merged back into the original architecture with zero inference overhead.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new parameter-efficient finetuning method for Continual Learning.
TailLoR uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame for low-rank updates.
It employs a soft spectral penalty to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, focusing adaptation on 'long-tail' features.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Addresses a critical challenge in humanoid robotics: bridging high-level task planning with low-level whole-body control.
Introduces 'HANDOFF,' a novel, compact, and explicit interface designed to translate task semantics into actionable control signals.
Utilizes distilled Complementary Teachers to achieve intuitive and generalizable control for diverse manipulation skills.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the progressive nature of human-AI co-editing, rather than just final outputs.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision workflows.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional drafting and revision processes.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically regarding sequential computation and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT achieves this by completely bypassing the need for recurrent credit propagation, aiming to improve long-range association learning.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage 'discarded' tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded, low-confidence tokens are shown to act as a valuable lookahead signal for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
The technique enhances the retrieval process by surfacing salient entities early in the denoising trajectory.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer designed to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomial preconditioning.
Crucially, the preconditioned weights can be merged back post-training, resulting in zero inference overhead.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the progressive nature of human-AI co-editing.
Introduces 'OpAI-Bench,' a new operation-guided benchmark designed to track how AI authorship signals change during revision workflows.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants in real-world professional drafting and revision processes.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method for self-augmenting retrieval specifically for Diffusion Language Models (DLMs).
DLMs generate text by iteratively denoising the entire response in parallel, predicting tentative tokens at each step.
The discarded, low-confidence tokens from the denoising process are shown to serve as a useful lookahead signal for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

stories.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
stories.author: Furu Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses the efficiency bottleneck in long-context inference for LLMs, particularly in reasoning tasks.
It tackles the common trade-off between speed (structured sparse methods) and accuracy (token sparse methods).
The proposed method, 'Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing,' aims to improve both decoding efficiency and quality.

stories.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
stories.author: Mehmet Iscan
bullet_points: The study investigates the effectiveness of 'prompt skills' (like instructing LLMs to act as Popperian falsificationists) in improving code generation.
A key concern raised is that performance gains are often measured using LLM-as-a-judge systems, which suffer from documented biases.
The research questions whether the observed improvements in code quality are genuinely due to the prompting technique or artifacts of the evaluation method.

stories.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
stories.author: Jiatao Gu
bullet_points: The paper proposes 'Latent Reasoning' as an alternative to explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting in LLMs.
Traditional textual CoT forces reasoning through a discrete, serial token stream, which limits computational bandwidth.
Latent reasoning aims to perform intermediate computation in a higher-bandwidth, non-verbalized manner.

stories.title: USAD 2.0: Scaling Representation Distillation for Universal Audio Understanding
stories.author: James Glass
bullet_points: Introduces USAD 2.0, a new universal audio encoder designed to handle diverse audio inputs.
Addresses the limitation of current domain-specific audio encoders by integrating knowledge from both Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and supervised methods.
Highlights the growing trend of using single encoders for diverse inputs in modern audio applications powered by LLMs.

stories.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
stories.author: Jiebo Luo
bullet_points: The research addresses the reliability of using LLMs to simulate user stances in online discussions.
It introduces 'counterfactual context revision' as a method to audit the accuracy of these LLM simulations.
The study investigates whether LLM simulations accurately reflect user beliefs or are overly sensitive to minor contextual changes.

stories.title: Zeroserve: A zero-config web server you can script with eBPF
stories.author: losfair
bullet_points: Introduces Zeroserve, a new web server designed for zero-configuration.
Key feature is the ability to script server logic using eBPF.
This combines modern networking technology (eBPF) with simplified server deployment.

stories.title: Run Coding Agents on Local AI — Zero Cloud, Full Control
stories.author: dalenguyen
bullet_points: The article discusses the use of coding agents (e.g., Codex CLI, Claude Code, Cursor, Pi) as productivity tools.
The core theme is running these AI agents locally, eliminating the need for cloud services.
This local execution offers users 'Full Control' over their coding AI workflow.

stories.title: We built a coding harness that beats frontier models using open ones. It's in open beta.
stories.author: jon_at_backboardio
bullet_points: A new 'coding harness' has been developed that aims to outperform frontier AI models.
The core philosophy behind the harness is prioritizing 'software memory' over model architecture.
The project is currently available in an open beta phase, suggesting community testing.

stories.title: SOC-in-a-Box: One LLM, Eight Hats, A Production-Bar AI SOC on a Single GPU
stories.author: vinayiitkgp
bullet_points: Presents a concept for an AI-powered Security Operations Center (SOC) consolidated into a single LLM.
The system simulates multiple specialized security roles ('Eight Hats') on a single GPU.
Addresses the complexity of real-world SOC operations by automating distinct functions like alert triage.

stories.title: Teaching a Reranker the Language of Security Tickets (+41% MRR@10)
stories.author: vinayiitkgp
bullet_points: The article details using a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline within a Security Operations Center (SOC).
The system is trained on a large corpus of historical security data (over 142,000 closed XSOAR tickets).
The application demonstrates a measurable business impact, achieving a +41% MRR@10.
